title: Day of the Week Calculator — Find Any Weekday
shortDescription: Enter any calendar date and instantly see which day of the week it falls on.
description: A free day of the week calculator that tells you the weekday for any date. Pick a calendar date and it shows whether it was a Monday, Tuesday, or any other day — perfect for checking past birthdays, planning events, or settling historical date questions.
metaDescription: Use this free day of the week calculator to find out which weekday any date falls on. Enter a date and instantly see Monday through Sunday for past or future dates.

result.label: Day of the week
result.empty: Pick a date to see which day of the week it falls on.

sections.heading: What this calculator does
paragraphs: This tool takes any calendar date and tells you exactly which day of the week it falls on, from Monday through Sunday. Whether the date is in the distant past or far in the future, the result appears instantly.
It is handy for confirming the weekday of a birthday, a historical event, a contract deadline, or a planned trip without flipping through a calendar.

sections.heading: How the weekday is determined
paragraphs: Every date in the Gregorian calendar maps to a fixed weekday following a repeating seven-day cycle. The calculator applies the standard calendar rules, including leap years, so the answer is always accurate.
Because the weekday name is shown in your own language by the browser, the result matches your regional settings automatically.

howTo.name: Choose a date
howTo.text: Click the date field and select the year, month, and day you want to check.

howTo.name: Read the result
howTo.text: The matching day of the week appears immediately below the input.

howTo.name: Try another date
howTo.text: Change the date at any time to look up a different weekday.

faq.q: Can I check dates far in the past?
faq.a: Yes. The calculator handles historical dates correctly using standard Gregorian calendar rules.

faq.q: Does it work for future dates?
faq.a: Absolutely. Pick any future date and it will show the weekday it will fall on.
Yes. Leap years are accounted for automatically, so February 29 dates are correct.

faq.q: In what language are the weekday names shown?
faq.a: Weekday names follow your browser and device language settings, so they appear in your own language.

faq.q: Is this tool free to use?
faq.a: Yes, it is completely free and requires no sign-up. Use it as many times as you like.
